body:
So if user have installation/deployment script, we should be able to convert any of such script to documentation installation/deploy guide.

If user have documentation and installation/deploy guide, we should be able to any GitHub repository convert such documentation into sh/powershell script

We should have at least 50 test cases for most popular GitHub project (taking their installation scripts only if they have prepared, and converting them to markdown docs similar to how they do their docs), and back from their docs to installation scripts similar to how they do their scripts.

Even better to take top 50 most popular GitHub projects that have both installation scripts and manual installation guides, and support them all by generalizing the algorithm.

And we also need a meta algorithm, the algorithm that constructs such algorithm by this specific task.

So we need to make sure our AI system is able to take this issue as input, and do all the actions necessary to produce an algorithm.

We need to push our universality and generalization at the highest possible level.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

Please plan and execute everything in this single pull request, you have unlimited time and context, as context auto-compacts and you can continue indefinitely, until it is each and every requirement fully addressed, and everything is totally done. Nothing should be delayed or deferred into separate pull requests or next working session, do everything in one go.
title: We need to support conversion between README.md files containing instructions on how to install something from GitHub Projects to sh/powershell scripts and back
